hm... I guess it's been years sine I tested this, but modules used to build just fine even with unexported symbols, you just cannot insert them because the symbol cannot be found at insertion time?
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

in fact I just checked and the way we check if a symbol is exported is by using the LB_CHECK_EXPORT define

Patch Set 2:

I pushed patch https://review.whamcloud.com/55562 that uses LB_CHECK_EXPORT(), to move this issue forward one way or the other.

Patch Set 2:

I see that this can be improved, since kallsyms_lookup_name() is also available
for this kernel we can still use the non-exported lock_page_memcg() and make 
use of the fast path in vvp_set_batch_dirty() in SUSE 15 SP6

Will confirm with some local testing..

Patch Set 2:

I hope SUSE can back port the large folio support in a future SP :)

parents.subject: LU-10522 utils: new --mindepth for lfs find

commit.subject: LU-17903 build: enable fast path of vvp_set_batch_dirty
commit.message:
LU-17903 build: enable fast path of vvp_set_batch_dirty

SUSE 15 SP6 6.4 kernel retains kallsyms_lookup_name so
the fast path of vvp_set_batch_dirty() can be enabled.

However the combination of kallsyms_lookup_name without
lock_page_memcg breaks some old assumptions

Prefer folio_memcg_lock to lock_page_memcg however

Linux commit v5.15-12272-g913ffbdd9985
  mm: unexport folio_memcg_{,un}lock

folio_memcg_lock is also not exported so use
kallsyms_lookup_name to acquire the symbol

HPE-bug-id: LUS-12371
Test-Parameters: trivial
Fixes: 61e83a6f130 ("LU-16113 build: Fix configure tests for lock_page_memcg")
Signed-off-by: Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>
Change-Id: I8ac6b7bde8ee8964db5a801c2f3c4dfb2ef459f9
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55300
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Petros Koutoupis <petros.koutoupis@hpe.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
